title: The beard before August
summary: A barber has not seen his regular customer for two months. The customer has been growing a beard for his daughter's wedding.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Tiago. Sit down. You did not come in May.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: I did not come in April either.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Two months. Is the beard a decision or an accident?

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: A decision.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Tell me.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: My daughter is getting married in August. She wants me to grow it. Her grandfather had a beard.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: How long is it now?

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: Six centimetres.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: It will be eight by August.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: How do you know?

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Tiago, I cut your grandfather's hair before you were born. I knew the back of his neck better than his wife did.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: I forgot.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Many people forget. Now. The beard needs help.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: What kind?

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: We make the line clear at the jaw. We shorten the moustache. We do not cut. We shape.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: I do not want to look different.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: You will look like a man who has thought about his beard for two months. Which you have.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: How long will this take?

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Twenty minutes.

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: Okay.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: One more question. Are you growing it after the wedding?

body.speaker: Tiago
body.text: I do not know.

body.speaker: Mr Faria
body.text: Decide before August. The man who shaves it the day after the wedding loses something. The man who keeps it for a year is a different man. Both are okay. Now lean back.

annotations.meaningVi: sắp cưới / đang chuẩn bị kết hôn (cụm cố định)
xăng-ti-mét (cách viết Anh Anh; Mỹ: centimeters)
ngả người ra sau (cụm động từ; lệnh thường dùng trong tiệm cắt tóc)
cạo râu (động từ chia ngôi thứ ba số ít)
cũng không (dùng cuối câu phủ định để đồng ý)
tình cờ / việc xảy ra ngoài ý muốn (không phải tai nạn giao thông ở đây)
mất đi điều gì đó (diễn đạt bóng bẩy, không chỉ mất vật)

grammarNotes.title: Past simple: questions and negatives with 'did'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để hỏi hoặc phủ định trong quá khứ đơn, A1 dùng trợ động từ 'did' với động từ nguyên thể. Trong bài: 'You did not come in May.' 'I did not come in April either.' 'I cut your grandfather's hair before you were born.' (khẳng định — không dùng 'did'). Lưu ý sau 'did not' thì động từ giữ nguyên — 'come', không phải 'came': 'did not come', 'did not know'. Đặt câu hỏi: 'Did you come?' / 'Where did you go?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Past simple questions and negatives use the auxiliary 'did' with the base verb. The dialogue: 'You did not come in May.' 'I did not come in April either.' After 'did not' the verb stays in its base form — 'come', not 'came'. Statements without 'did' use the past form directly: 'I cut your grandfather's hair…' Questions: 'Did you come?' / 'Where did you go?'

translationVi:
Ông Faria: Tiago. Ngồi đi. Tháng Năm cậu không đến.
Tiago: Tháng Tư cháu cũng không đến.
Ông Faria: Hai tháng. Bộ râu này là quyết định hay là tình cờ?
Tiago: Quyết định.
Ông Faria: Kể đi.
Tiago: Con gái cháu cưới tháng Tám. Nó muốn cháu để râu. Ông ngoại nó có râu.
Ông Faria: Giờ dài bao nhiêu?
Tiago: Sáu centimet.
Ông Faria: Đến tháng Tám sẽ là tám.
Tiago: Sao bác biết?
Ông Faria: Tiago à, bác cắt tóc cho ông ngoại cháu từ trước khi cháu ra đời. Bác biết gáy ông ấy còn rõ hơn vợ ông ấy.
Tiago: Cháu quên.
Ông Faria: Nhiều người quên. Giờ thì. Bộ râu này cần giúp.
Tiago: Kiểu gì ạ?
Ông Faria: Mình làm đường viền rõ ở hàm. Mình cắt ngắn ria mép. Mình không cắt râu. Mình tạo dáng.
Tiago: Cháu không muốn trông khác.
Ông Faria: Cháu sẽ trông như một người đàn ông đã suy nghĩ về bộ râu của mình suốt hai tháng. Mà đúng là vậy.
Tiago: Mất bao lâu ạ?
Ông Faria: Hai mươi phút.
Tiago: Vâng.
Ông Faria: Một câu cuối. Sau đám cưới cháu có để tiếp không?
Tiago: Cháu không biết.
Ông Faria: Quyết trước tháng Tám. Người cạo râu một ngày sau đám cưới mất một thứ. Người để râu thêm một năm là một người khác. Cả hai đều ổn. Giờ ngả đầu ra sau.

questions.prompt: How long has Tiago not come to the barber?
questions.explanation: 'Two months. Is the beard a decision or an accident?'

questions.prompt: Is the beard a decision or an accident?
Why is Tiago growing the beard?
options: His daughter is getting married in August; her grandfather had a beard, and she wants Tiago to look like him.
questions.hint: Tiago's longer reply about his daughter.

questions.prompt: How long is the beard now?
How does Mr Faria know about Tiago's grandfather?
options: He cut his hair before Tiago was born — he knew the back of the grandfather's neck better than his wife did.
questions.hint: Mr Faria's reply about the past.

questions.prompt: What does Mr Faria say the beard needs?
options: Making the line clear at the jaw, shortening the moustache, and shaping — not cutting.
questions.hint: Look at the 'help' Mr Faria describes.

options: No — he will look like a man who has thought about his beard for two months
questions.hint: Mr Faria's reply about looking different.

questions.prompt: How long will the work take?
What advice does Mr Faria give about after the wedding?
options: Decide before August whether to keep it. The day-after shaver loses something. The year-long keeper is a different man. Both are okay; the one who doesn't decide looks unhappy in photos.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: A small, repeated relationship with a tradesman can hold and return a piece of family memory that the family itself has half-forgotten.
Daughters should not give beard advice.
questions.explanation: Mr Faria remembers the grandfather Tiago has forgotten — and lengthens the beard accordingly.
questions.hint: Think about who in the dialogue actually remembers the grandfather best.

cloze.template:
Mr Faria: Tiago. Sit down. You did not come in {{b1}}.
Tiago: I did not come in April either.
Mr Faria: Two months. Is the {{b2}} a decision or an accident?
Tiago: A decision.
Mr Faria: Tell me.
Tiago: My daughter is getting married in {{b3}}. She wants me to grow it. Her grandfather had a beard.
Mr Faria: How long is it now?
Tiago: {{b4}} centimetres.
Mr Faria: It will be eight by August.
Tiago: How do you know?
Mr Faria: Tiago, I cut your {{b5}}'s hair before you were born. I knew the back of his neck better than his wife did.
Tiago: I forgot.
Mr Faria: Many people forget. Now. The beard needs help.
Tiago: What kind?
Mr Faria: We make the line clear at the {{b6}}. We shorten the moustache. We do not cut. We {{b7}}.
Tiago: I do not want to look different.
Mr Faria: You will look like a man who has thought about his beard for {{b8}} months. Which you have.
Tiago: How long will this take?
Mr Faria: {{b9}} minutes.
Tiago: Okay.
Mr Faria: One more question. Are you growing it after the wedding?
Tiago: I do not know.
Mr Faria: Decide before August. The man who shaves it the day after the wedding loses something. The man who keeps it for a year is a different man. Both are okay. Now lean {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: He did not come in May.
We do not cut. We shape.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Faria remembers Tiago's grandfather better than Tiago does. Is there someone outside your family — a barber, a teacher, a shopkeeper — who knows or remembers something about your family that you yourself have lost track of? What does that person quietly preserve, and what would it take to ask them?
